design.employment_pool_note: 2022_Apr excluded from employment pooling due to COVID pre-trend violation (placebo Î²=+0.296, p=0.018). Wage results retain 2022 (wage placebo passes).

events.label: 2025_Jan
events.note: MW increase enacted by DS 006-2024-TR, effective January 1, 2025. No EPE data available yet; causal estimates pending 2025 EPE release.

kaitz.kaitz_formal_basis: 2022 EPE formal median S/1,500 as denominator
kaitz.note: Kaitz_formal=0.753 is the highest in modern Peru history (2003-2022 range: 0.500-0.635)

model_prediction.source: Univariate OLS: epsilon ~ Kaitz_formal, n=9 events (2003-2022)
caveats: Kaitz_formal=0.753 is outside the historical estimation range (0.500-0.635)
Prediction involves out-of-sample extrapolation; treat as directional only
Pooled employment elasticity from 8 events (ex-2022): epsilon=-0.051, p=0.714 (not significant)

policy_current.note: Highest Kaitz in modern history. Causal effects TBD pending 2025 EPE data (expected late 2025 / early 2026).
policy_current.paper_framing: We study 9 MW events spanning 2003-2022. As of January 2025, Peru enacted a 10th increase (DS 006-2024-TR, S/1,025 to S/1,130, +10.2%), pushing the Kaitz index to an unprecedented 0.753. Our historical estimates provide directional evidence, but the current bindingness (Kaitz=0.753) exceeds our entire estimation sample (0.500-0.635). Effects of the 2025 increase remain to be estimated once EPE data become available.
policy_current.columna_hook: El salario mínimo peruano es hoy más alto en términos reales que en cualquier momento de la historia moderna — y está en terreno inexplorado. 20 años de datos nos dicen qué ocurre con Kaitz entre 0.50 y 0.63; en 0.75 no hay precedente histórico en el Perú.
